_comment: UK direct debit failure rate — share of direct debits returned unpaid due to insufficient funds. Sourced from ONS 'Monthly Direct Debit failure rate and average transaction amount' dataset (Pay.UK / Vocalink data, ONS calculations). Series used: Table 1, 'DD Failure Rates Total NSA' (all categories, non-seasonally adjusted, total).

methodology.indicator: BACS-administered direct debit failure rate, monthly, % of total direct debit transactions, total all categories, non-seasonally adjusted (NSA)
methodology.release_cadence: monthly dataset release accompanying the ONS 'Economic activity and social change in the UK, real-time indicators' bulletin (typically published mid-month, reporting data through ~6 weeks earlier)
methodology.extraction_notes: All 21 monthly values extracted directly from sheet '1.DD Failure Rates Total NSA' of the ONS xlsx published 23 April 2026 (data coverage Jan 2021 – Mar 2026, file: directdebittransactionsandfailuresdataset230426.xlsx). Source values are decimal fractions (e.g. 0.0214 = 2.14%); rounded to one decimal in this fixture. The xlsx is republished each month with revisions, so the latest edition is the canonical source for the entire historical series — each point's URL therefore points to the dataset landing page rather than the specific edition in which the month first appeared. The 'release' / 'published' fields identify the bulletin release in which each month was first reported. ANCHOR DISCREPANCY: the live fixture packages/data-sources/src/fixtures/ons-rti.json reports 1.8% for April 2026 (observed 2026-04-10), but the ONS Total NSA series shows 2.41% for March 2026 (the most recent month available as of the 23 April 2026 release; April 2026 data is not yet published). The 1.8% figure does not correspond to any month in the Total NSA series for the period covered here — closest matches are sub-categories such as Mortgages (~0.85–1.2%) or Water (~1.0–1.2%), neither of which is the headline figure. Recommend reconciling the live fixture against this authoritative series before launch.
